ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: Gɔ ɔnya Yesu dzoroo ne, ɔtere ɔba ɔ̃apɛ agɛgɛ̃ i ɔ̃ katɔ̃.
translated: When Jesus saw him, he knew he had been sick for a long time.

ref: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
text: Si ɔrere ɔbara-ɔ itɔ̃me sɔ si ɔfɔ fɔ awu wa apia asɛ karɔ̃ ne, ɖi nwa apia asia kato ana tã wũ.
translated: If a stranger's blood is shed and we are called to come and see, we should not go and see.

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Ɣɛɛ atɔ̃me wa nsɛ abɔrɛ i ɔturi kanya ame ne, ɔ̃ ɔtu ame abɔrɛ fiɛ asɛ apukutura wũ.
translated: If a person does not treat you with the kindness you deserve, you are not obligated to treat them with kindness either.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Ne manɔ silɔ ɔbɔrɛ i kato siɣɛ sɔ, “Wũ Ɔbi gɔ loisɛ lopia kuɣɛ i ngbe ne, ɔbo me anɔ gbaã.”
translated: If a person does not know how to pound fufu, they say, "The mortar belonging to Obi is crooked, that is why I cannot pound properly."

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, mala kulu maɣɛ sɔ, “Fɔ Ɣaa Ɔbi, be mpia i bo gu-ɔ ndɛ̃? Ɔba ɔɔba sɔ aba aakpadzɛ̃ bo kutsue aɖegu ɔwi gɔ masɛ katɔ̃?”
translated: Oluwe said, "I am going to ask Gaa Obi, why is it that he doesn't come? Is it that the child he has given birth to has grown feathers and wings that he doesn't come again?"
